notes: Cross-referenced validation of all routes using the Coastal Road / Roxas Boulevard corridor (Roxas Blvd, Diosdado Macapagal Blvd, Coastal Road R-1 Expressway). Covers Navotas/R-10 north through Luneta, Malate, CCP, MOA/Bay City, DFA, ASEANA, PITX. Sources: 16 raw JSON files.

routes.route_name: EDSA Carousel — Coastal / Roxas Blvd Segment (Heritage Hotel ↔ PITX)
routes.destination: Heritage Hotel / MRT Taft (Pasay Rotonda) — southern coastal segment only
key_stops: Ayala Malls Manila Bay / ASEANA (NB only)
SM Mall of Asia / Roxas Blvd Eastbound (SB) / SM Mall of Asia (NB)
routes.frequency_notes: Peak: ~2-5 min headway. Off-peak: ~5-15 min. Late night (11PM-4AM): Taft-Monumento limited service only (coastal segment suspended overnight). Full service 4AM-11PM daily.
routes.geometry_source: OSM relation likely exists; route follows Roxas Blvd/Diosdado Macapagal Blvd on coastal approach to PITX
routes.notes: This entry covers only the coastal segment of the full Monumento–PITX route. Southern 5km of the Carousel runs on Roxas Blvd / Macapagal / Coastal Road, not EDSA. Operators: Mega Manila Consortium Corp + ES Transport (87 operators, 751 buses). Full route: Monumento→PITX (₱75.50 max). Southbound uses SM MOA/Roxas Eastbound stop; northbound uses Roxas Blvd Westbound stop — different curbside locations due to road geometry. Mixed-traffic (no bus lane) on coastal approach.

routes.route_name: City Bus Route 23: Plaza Lawton–Alabang via Coastal Road
routes.geometry_source: OSM (LTFRB PUB network); OSM route_master available
routes.notes: Operator: Alabang Transport Service Cooperative. Only confirmed city bus route using Roxas Blvd as a through-corridor between Manila and Alabang. Distinct from Route 24 (Lawton→Alabang via SLEx). OSM relation confirms route. LTFRB route code: MM-BUS-023 per ltoportal.

routes.route_name: City Bus Route 44: Alabang–Navotas via Sucat Road, Roxas Blvd
routes.notes: Operator: Alabang Metro Link. Long north-south route spanning the full NCR coastal axis. LTFRB route code: MM-BUS-044 per ltoportal and wikimili. Exact routing through Manila is unclear — may use Roxas Blvd proper or a parallel (Taft Ave, A. Mabini). 'Via Roxas Blvd' stated in OSM tags.

routes.frequency_notes: Not documented; serves as EDSA alternative
routes.notes: Documented in transit-blogs-routes (blog-2) and city-bus-operators-routes. Uses Radial Road 10 (R-10) and Coastal Road as EDSA Carousel alternative. Multiple operators may run this. The corridor is distinct from Roxas Blvd proper: R-10 runs through the Port Area / North Harbor area, connecting to the coastal axis south of Intramuros.

routes.route_name: Love Bus Route 52: VGC–PITX via Dimasalang & Roxas Blvd
routes.frequency_notes: Zero-fare electric bus; schedule not documented
routes.notes: Operated by Global Electric Transport (GET Philippines) as Love Bus (zero-fare community transit). wikimili-routes lists this route alignment. Route uses Roxas Blvd as the Manila-to-PITX coastal approach. Operational status uncertain — Love Bus L1 (VGC–Batasan) and L2 (VGC–Fairview) confirmed; VGC–PITX variant's current operational status unverified.

LTFRB T-code: T429. Operator: Alabang Transport Service Cooperative (confirmed in OSM Wiki Metro Manila Jeepney routes). Uses Coastal Road (R-1 Expressway). High-volume route connecting Muntinlupa to Pasay via coastal bypass. Related to Baclaran-UV-Alabang UV Express route on same corridor.

routes.route_name: Baclaran–SM Mall of Asia via Coastal Road
routes.destination: SM Mall of Asia (MOA), Bay City, Pasay
routes.notes: Short feeder connecting Baclaran hub to SM MOA. Confirmed in baclaran-terminal and pasay-edsa-terminal analyses. High ridership due to MOA employment and leisure trips. Separate from T429 which continues to Alabang.

routes.frequency_notes: Daily; multiple options — traditional ₱11 / modern ₱13; also EDSA Carousel with MOA signboard variant
routes.notes: Short intra-Pasay route from MRT Taft to MOA via Malibay. Multiple vehicle types serve this O/D. Documented in pasay-edsa-terminal analysis. ₱11 traditional; ₱13 modern PUJ fare.

routes.route_name: Baclaran–Lawton via SM Mall of Asia
routes.notes: Single source (lrt1-feeder-routes analysis). Route likely uses Diosdado Macapagal Blvd / Roxas Blvd for the MOA→Lawton segment. Serves as feeder to both Baclaran and Central Terminal LRT-1 stations. No T-code or Sakay ID found.

routes.frequency_notes: Unknown; pioneer route launched 2018 by SETSCO cooperative
routes.notes: Modern PUJ route from 2018 PUVMP pioneer program. Connects government district (Senate, GSIS) via MOA to PITX along Diosdado Macapagal Blvd / Coastal Road. No current operational confirmation after COVID-period PUVMP disruptions. LTFRB MPUJ route code: MPUJ-035 per modernization analysis.

routes.route_name: Dasmariñas Resettlement Area–Baclaran via Coastal Road
routes.notes: Cross-boundary jeepney from Cavite province into NCR via Coastal Road. Documented in baclaran-terminal analysis. Dasmariñas Resettlement is a large informal settlement area in the coastal zone of Cavite. Route serves significant informal sector commuters. No T-code confirmed.

routes.origin: CCP Complex, Pasay / Roxas Blvd junction
routes.notes: Mentioned in lrt1-feeder-routes analysis as connecting the cultural district (CCP) to LRT-1. Uses Roxas Blvd for the short (~1 km) hop to Vito Cruz LRT-1 station. No T-code, Sakay ID, or operator name found.

routes.route_name: Baclaran–Alabang UV Express via Coastal Road
routes.notes: Moovit UV Express agency 1450948 confirmed. AC van/UV Express. Fare: ₱13 base + ₱1.80/km (UV Express standard). Travel time ~20-30 min via Coastal Road. Related to T429 (jeepney) on same corridor. Reverse trip (Alabang→Baclaran) also confirmed.

routes.route_name: Market! Market! (BGC) → SM Mall of Asia UV Express
routes.origin: Market! Market! UV Express Terminal, Taguig (BGC)
routes.notes: Moovit confirmed (route 9074416). Connects BGC to MOA. Likely uses C-5 then coastal approach to MOA rather than Roxas Blvd proper. Serves cross-corridor BGC→Bay City commuters.

routes.route_name: Festival Mall/Alabang → SM Mall of Asia UV Express
routes.notes: Moovit confirmed (route 8750843). Festival Mall is in Alabang area, south of the standard Baclaran-UV-Alabang terminal. Uses Coastal Road corridor.

routes.route_name: MOA (SM Mall of Asia) → Imus, Cavite UV Express
routes.origin: SM Mall of Asia van stop, Pasay
key_stops: SM Mall of Asia van terminal
routes.notes: SM MOA van terminal serves Imus, Cavite at ₱100 flat rate. Hours: 10AM–10PM. Documented in pasay-edsa-terminal analysis. Southbound Coastal Road departure from MOA area.

routes.origin: NAIA Terminal 3, Arrival Area Bay 12, Parañaque
routes.frequency_notes: 12 trips each way daily; 4:30AM–8PM from NAIA T3; 4:45AM–7:45PM from PITX
routes.notes: UBE Express P2P service. ₱150 flat fare. Does not use Roxas Blvd proper — NAIA to PITX route uses airport roads. Included as PITX-coastal corridor adjacent service for completeness.

routes.route_name: UBE Express: NAIA T3 ↔ Ayala Malls Manila Bay [SUSPENDED]
routes.destination: Ayala Malls Manila Bay, Parañaque (near ASEANA)
routes.notes: This route directly used the coastal corridor (NAIA to Ayala Malls Manila Bay via coastal road). Suspended as of 2025. Included for record; should not be included in active GTFS output.
